_comment: Sweep routers (#693/PR #711): a lone player Cavalry ('Sweeper', uid 0) sits between two enemy targets. 'Holdout' (uid 3) is a full-strength Infantry that closes on a player Spearmen tie-down ('Anvil') and stays FIGHTING it -- close to the sweeper (~140-150 px) but never routs. 'Fleeing' (uid 4) is a weak, low-morale Infantry spawned in contact with a second player Cavalry ('Breaker'); it breaks almost immediately (ROUTING within the first few ticks) and keeps fleeing, ending up farther from the sweeper than Holdout. Giving the sweeper the SWEEP_ROUTERS stance (Ctrl+,) makes it lock onto the more distant ROUTING Fleeing and chase it, bypassing the closer, still-fighting Holdout -- the actual re-targeting choice under test. Verified by state dump: from tick ~15 onward target_enemy_uid stays 4 (Fleeing) throughout, never 3 (Holdout), even though Holdout is consistently the nearer of the two.
